description: A designed slogan graphic built over a sepia/duotone-toned photograph. The photo depicts an arrest in progress: a man (back to camera, face not shown) wearing a dark sweater and light khaki pants stands handcuffed behind his back, being held by an officer in a dark tactical jacket whose back reads 'POLICE FEDERAL' (suggesting a foreign/federal police force). They stand beside a dark SUV in a paved outdoor area; a tree is visible upper left. The whole image is rendered in a brown/sepia monochrome to give a gritty, serious tone. In the lower-left a solid blue rectangle box carries large white bold serif text reading 'Making America Safe Again.' No agency seal is visible on the image itself. The propaganda technique pairs an anonymized criminal-arrest scene with the administration's signature slogan to frame immigration enforcement as public-safety action; the cropped/anonymous subject lets it stand in for any 'criminal alien.' Target audience: general public and immigrants, messaging that lawbreakers will be caught and arrested. Tweet context says USCIS Houston helped apprehend an alien connected to a Terroristic Threats incident in partnership with ICE.
